accept: Skill exists at ~/.claude/skills/act/SKILL.md with a description that triggers on '.act/' / 'act repo' / 'act task tracker' / agent-workflow keywords.
Skill content covers what the act repo's CLAUDE.md currently covers, generalised away from this-repo specifics.
act repo's CLAUDE.md is reduced to project-specific overrides + a pointer to the global skill (not a duplicate).
Tested by dropping act into a fresh repo and confirming a new CC session correctly uses act_next + the canonical loop.
payload.description:
After 3-5 successful sessions of dogfooding (we have at least 4), the stable patterns from CLAUDE.md should extract into a global skill. The skill triggers on the presence of .act/ in the project root and contains: the canonical loop, halt conditions, sub-agent isolation rules, commit discipline, dep semantics. Once installed, dropping act into any new repo means agents auto-pick-up the workflow without copy-pasting CLAUDE.md.

The act repo's CLAUDE.md becomes a thin layer on top of the skill — just project-specific deviations and rationale.

Skill format: ~/.claude/skills/act/SKILL.md with frontmatter triggering on .act/ presence. Test installation in a fresh repo to validate trigger.
payload.title: global Claude Code skill that triggers on presence of .act/
